title: Age at onset of mental disorders worldwide: large-scale meta-analysis of 192 epidemiological studies
finding_one_line: Schizophrenia-spectrum onset peaks at 20.5 years with only ~3% before age 14 — late-adolescent/early-adult emergence, near-absent in childhood.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: peak age of onset for schizophrenia-spectrum
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: schizophrenia-spectrum disorders/primary psychotic states: 3%, 12.3%, 47.8%, 20.5 years

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: proportion of onset before age 14
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: schizophrenia-spectrum disorders/primary psychotic states: 3%, 12.3%, 47.8%, 20.5 years

claims_it_can_support: Onset clusters late adolescence/early adulthood
Childhood-onset rare (~3% before 14)

title: Implications of normal brain development for the pathogenesis of schizophrenia
finding_one_line: Foundational neurodevelopmental model: an early fixed lesion interacts silently with normal later maturation, so symptoms appear only when affected regions mature in adolescence/early adulthood.
claims_it_can_support: Neurodevelopmental hypothesis: early insult, late clinical expression

title: Genetic insights into the neurodevelopmental origins of schizophrenia
finding_one_line: Modern genetics-era revisit: gene-expression/epigenetic analyses of post-mortem cortex indicate early brain development mediates genetic risk.
claims_it_can_support: Neurodevelopmental hypothesis supported by modern molecular evidence

title: Schizophrenia risk from complex variation of complement component 4
finding_one_line: The strongest population-level genetic association (MHC) arises partly from C4 alleles; higher C4A expression tracks higher risk, and C4 mediates synapse elimination in mice — excessive complement-driven pruning.
claims_it_can_support: Complement C4 increases risk
C4 mediates synaptic pruning
Links strongest GWAS signal to excessive pruning
notes: abstract has no numeric effect size; supports mechanism narrative, not a number.

title: Extraordinary neoteny of synaptic spines in the human prefrontal cortex
journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the USA
finding_one_line: Human prefrontal spine overproduction and pruning continue into the third decade before stabilizing — synaptic elimination is protracted through the window when schizophrenia emerges.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: period over which prefrontal pruning continues before adult level
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: overproduction and developmental remodeling...continues beyond adolescence and throughout the third decade of life before stabilizing at the adult level.

claims_it_can_support: Pruning window overlaps age of schizophrenia onset

title: Schizophrenia as a complex trait: evidence from a meta-analysis of twin studies
finding_one_line: Meta-analysis of 12 twin studies: heritability of liability 81% (95% CI 73-90%), shared-environment 11%.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: heritability of liability to schizophrenia
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: the point estimate of heritability in liability to schizophrenia was 81% (95% confidence interval, 73%-90%).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: shared/common environmental contribution
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: consistent evidence across these studies for common or shared environmental influences on liability to schizophrenia-joint estimate, 11% (95% confidence interval, 3%-19%).

claims_it_can_support: Heritability roughly 80%
Genes and shared environment both contribute

title: Heritability of Schizophrenia and Schizophrenia Spectrum Based on the Nationwide Danish Twin Register
finding_one_line: Nationwide Danish twin register (31,524 pairs): heritability 79%; MZ concordance only 33% vs 7% DZ — genes dominate but do not fully determine illness.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: heritability of schizophrenia (Danish cohort)
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: We estimated the heritability of SZ to be 79%.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: probandwise concordance monozygotic vs dizygotic
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The probandwise concordance rate of SZ is 33% in monozygotic twins and 7% in dizygotic twins.

claims_it_can_support: Heritability ~79%
Identical-twin concordance only ~33% — genes not destiny

title: Mapping genomic loci implicates genes and synaptic biology in schizophrenia
finding_one_line: PGC3 GWAS (~76,755 cases, 243,649 controls): common-variant associations at 287 distinct loci, 120 genes prioritized — highly polygenic, no single causal gene.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: genome-wide-significant common-variant loci
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: we report common variant associations at 287 distinct genomic loci.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: genes prioritized as likely underpinning associations
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: we identify 120 genes (106 protein-coding) that are likely to underpin associations at some of these loci.

claims_it_can_support: Highly polygenic (287 loci)
No single 'schizophrenia gene'
notes: Trubetskoy abstractText truncated in Europe PMC JSON by ~7000-author authorString; identifiers confirmed via Europe PMC REST, abstract sentences from NCBI efetch for PMID 35396580.

title: The dopamine hypothesis of schizophrenia: version III--the final common pathway
finding_one_line: Dopamine hypothesis version III: diverse risk factors converge on increased presynaptic striatal dopamine, driving psychosis via aberrant salience; antipsychotics act downstream.
claims_it_can_support: Dopamine version III / final common pathway
Presynaptic striatal dysregulation is the core lesion
Aberrant salience
Antipsychotics act downstream

title: The nature of dopamine dysfunction in schizophrenia and what this means for treatment
finding_one_line: Meta-analysis of 44 imaging studies (618 patients): largest dopaminergic abnormality is presynaptic (Cohen d=0.79), only minor D2/3 elevation (d=0.26, absent in drug-naive) — D2-blocking drugs miss the presynaptic lesion.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: effect size for elevated presynaptic striatal dopaminergic function
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: There was a highly significant elevation (P.<001) in presynaptic dopaminergic function in schizophrenia with a large effect size (Cohen d=0.79).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: small D2/3 receptor availability elevation (not in drug-naive)
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: There was a small elevation in D(2/3) receptor availability (Cohen d=0.26), but this was not evident in drug-naive patients.

claims_it_can_support: Large-effect presynaptic dopamine elevation
Abnormality is presynaptic not receptor-level
D2-blocking drugs miss the real target (tension setup)

title: Schizophrenia, Dopamine and the Striatum: From Biology to Symptoms
finding_one_line: Updated review: greatest dopaminergic dysfunction is in nigrostriatal pathways implicating the dorsal (not mesolimbic) striatum.
claims_it_can_support: Modern dopamine account centered on dorsal/associative striatum

title: From revolution to evolution: the glutamate hypothesis of schizophrenia and its implication for treatment
finding_one_line: Glutamate/NMDA-hypofunction account: NMDAR antagonists reproduce schizophrenia-like symptoms; a co-equal, non-dopaminergic mechanism affecting positive AND negative symptoms.
claims_it_can_support: Glutamate/NMDA hypofunction as a co-equal mechanistic account
NMDAR antagonists induce schizophrenia-like symptoms

title: Subanesthetic effects of the noncompetitive NMDA antagonist, ketamine, in humans. Psychotomimetic, perceptual, cognitive, and neuroendocrine responses
finding_one_line: Landmark human challenge: subanesthetic ketamine (NMDA antagonist) produced BOTH positive and negative schizophrenia-like symptoms plus perceptual/cognitive impairments.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: ketamine reproduces positive AND negative symptoms
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Ketamine (1) produced behaviors similar to the positive and negative symptoms of schizophrenia; (2) elicited alterations in perception; (3) impaired performance on tests of vigilance, verbal fluency, and the Wisconsin Card Sorting Test.

claims_it_can_support: NMDA antagonism reproduces full symptom spectrum incl. negative/cognitive
Dopamine-only accounts insufficient

title: Childhood adversities increase the risk of psychosis: a meta-analysis of patient-control, prospective- and cross-sectional cohort studies
finding_one_line: Meta-analysis (36 studies): childhood adversity roughly triples psychosis risk (OR 2.78, 95% CI 2.34-3.31), ~33% population attributable risk.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: odds ratio linking childhood adversity to psychosis
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: with an overall effect of OR = 2.78 (95% CI = 2.34-3.31).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: population attributable risk from childhood adversity
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The estimated population attributable risk was 33% (16%-47%).

claims_it_can_support: Childhood adversity roughly triples psychosis risk (OR ~2.8)

title: Meta-analysis of the Association Between the Level of Cannabis Use and Risk of Psychosis
finding_one_line: Meta-analysis (66,816 individuals): dose-response — heaviest cannabis users have ~4-fold increased odds of psychosis (OR 3.90, 95% CI 2.84-5.34).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: odds of psychosis for heaviest cannabis users vs nonusers
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: A logistic regression model gave an OR of 3.90 (95% CI 2.84 to 5.34) for the risk of schizophrenia and other psychosis-related outcomes among the heaviest cannabis users compared to the nonusers.

claims_it_can_support: Cannabis-psychosis link is dose-dependent; heaviest use ~quadruples odds

title: Meta-analysis of the association of urbanicity with schizophrenia
finding_one_line: Meta-analysis: linear urbanicity gradient — risk in most urban environment is 2.37 times higher than most rural.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: relative risk most-urban vs most-rural
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The risk for schizophrenia at the most urban environment was estimated to be 2.37 times higher than in the most rural environment.

claims_it_can_support: Urban upbringing raises risk (~2.4x)

title: Schizophrenia and migration: a meta-analysis and review
finding_one_line: Meta-analysis: first-generation migrants have a mean weighted relative risk of 2.7 (95% CI 2.3-3.2).

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: relative risk among first-generation migrants
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: The mean weighted relative risk for developing schizophrenia among first-generation migrants (40 effect sizes) was 2.7 (95% CI=2.3-3.2).

claims_it_can_support: Migration/minority status raises risk (~2.7x)

title: Moderation of the effect of adolescent-onset cannabis use on adult psychosis by a functional polymorphism in the catechol-O-methyltransferase gene: longitudinal evidence of a gene X environment interaction
finding_one_line: Dunedin cohort G×E: COMT Val158 carriers most likely to develop psychotic symptoms after adolescent cannabis use, whereas Met/Met were unaffected — genotype moderates the environmental risk.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: COMT genotype moderates cannabis effect
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Carriers of the COMT valine158 allele were most likely to exhibit psychotic symptoms and to develop schizophreniform disorder if they used cannabis.

claims_it_can_support: Concrete G×E (COMT x cannabis); neither gene nor environment alone sufficient

title: Why has it taken so long for biological psychiatry to develop clinical tests and what to do about it?
finding_one_line: Authoritative admission: patients show many biological abnormalities but few yield any test with clinical utility — no validated diagnostic biomarker for schizophrenia.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: no validated diagnostic biomarker; diagnosis remains clinical
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Patients with mental disorders show many biological abnormalities which distinguish them from normal volunteers; however, few of these have led to tests with clinical utility.

claims_it_can_support: No validated diagnostic biomarker or blood/imaging test

title: The schizophrenogenic mother concept in American psychiatry
finding_one_line: Historical analysis: the 'schizophrenogenic mother' etiology was downgraded by its own former promulgators from a 'half truth' to a psychiatric 'myth' — the theory existed and was refuted.

numbers_it_licenses.what_it_is: the theory existed and was later labeled a myth
numbers_it_licenses.abstract_sentence: Though former promulgators have gone from calling the schizophrenogenic mother a "half truth" to a psychiatric "myth," this psychiatric concept remains a puzzle.

claims_it_can_support: MythVsFact: 'schizophrenogenic mother' was real historical concept, discredited
